column2: Text available in Perseus Digital Library GitHub (PerseusDL/canonical-greekLit or canonical-latinLit)
Text NOT available in Perseus Digital Library GitHub
PARTIALLY available — some works from the Loeb volume are in Perseus but not all
Thesaurus Linguae Graecae identifiers (e.g. tlg0012 = Homer)
Packard Humanities Institute identifiers for Latin (e.g. phi0690 = Virgil)
Whether the original Greek/Latin text exists in Perseus GitHub
Whether an English translation exists in Perseus GitHub (usually older PD translations)
Total: ~558 Loeb volumes. This spreadsheet aims to cover all of them.
Perseus availability may change. Data reflects approximate state as of early 2026.
Perseus texts are different editions/translations than the Loeb (usually older public domain versions).
Some Loeb volumes contain multiple authors; Perseus may have some but not all.
Fragment collections are generally NOT in Perseus.
Newer Loeb editions (2000s+) generally have different translations than Perseus.
Perseus English translations are typically older (pre-1930) public domain, not Loeb translations.
Late Latin authors (Augustine, Boethius, Bede, Claudian, Prudentius, Macrobius) mostly absent from Perseus.
Some LCL numbers have been reused for revised editions (old/new numbering coexists).
